title: The Four C's AI Assistant
voiceScript: If your AI assistant lives in a chat tab, it's not an assistant, it's a search box. I learned this the hard way. I wired everything on day one and shipped a mess my team refused to use. It forgot the client and gave me confident nonsense. So now I build it in four layers, in order. Context, then Connections, then Capabilities, then Cadence. The shift is real. Four C's, one layer at a time, zero wrong updates sent. Here's why it matters. One wrong update costs more than a week of setup. If you built yours this week, which C are you missing? Follow for more.

scenes.voiceText: If you built yours this week, which C are you missing? Follow for more.
scenes.cta: Which C are you missing right now?

source: LinkedIn post + 'Section 18: Build Your Personal AI Assistant' guide. The Four C's framework for building a personal AI assistant, in order: Context (Projects, custom instructions, reference docs, memory), Connections (Gmail/Calendar/Drive/Slack/Notion via MCP), Capabilities (Skills + Cowork to execute tasks), Cadence (local schedules + Cloud Routines, e.g. 7:30am morning brief). Build incrementally: one Project, one connection, one Skill, one routine. Failure mode of building everything day one: assistant forgets context, pulls wrong doc, produces confident nonsense, team refuses to use it. The cost of getting it wrong is trust (one wrong client update > a week of setup).
